heaviest_rainfall.status: Record amounts of rain fell over 115.3 hours today with a total accumulation of 38.67mm.  It fell at 2.2 mm per hour at times and ranked first from our records.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 27.0mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 115.3 hours on 09 November 2025 (ranking highest from our records for the longest storm) and it produced a total of 38.67mm of rain at a maximum intensity of 2.2mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm around.  It's the first significant stormy weather event since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest rainy periods recorded.  This rainy period is ranked 9th since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 07 November 2025 and has lasted 8 days.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 6 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (185°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 5%) and rainfall (up 0.2 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 356 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 444 m), rainfall (down 0.2 mm), feels like temperature (down 2.4°C), and outside temperature (down 2.2°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 355.7m lower than yesterday, 232.2m lower than the past week, 443.5m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 637m and a high of 1658m, averaging 597.7m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.
cloud levels.summary: Cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and very little to no wind will be present. Chance of light rain, cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions, and dim or diffuse light conditions. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.
